title: bloat-check: always allow on treadmill
body:
If we fail the bloat check, we can't test for more serious problems.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Who is looking at the nightly cron job? So far I heard nobody notifying anyone about any problems.

Renovate is now filling updates for these deps daily, I look at PR s see the test failure and fix it if possible or ping the person who can. Point in case (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18350), a regression was caught and fixed in c/common quickly.

So my question why not just remove this nightly job if nobody pays close attention when there is a IMO better solution already in place?
Thanks for the update. I've deleted the treadmill job - it never worked out anyway.
